# Test that drive line intersection vertices land exactly on graph edges,
# regardless of whether the drive line endpoint is before or after the edge.

polygon outer
0,0
300,0
300,200
0,200
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

generate
----
total_stalls: 208

# Dump the auto graph to identify the vertical edge we'll cross.
# At 90° default angle, interior aisles run vertically.
# Vertical edge at x≈180.3 between vertices 7 (180.3,39.9) and 8 (180.3,160.1).
graph
----
0: 0,0
1: 300,0
2: 300,200
3: 0,200
4: 279,0
5: 279,200
6: 223,0
7: 223,200
8: 167,0
9: 167,200
10: 112,0
11: 112,200
12: 56,0
13: 56,200
14: 300,191
15: 0,191
16: 279,191
17: 223,191
18: 167,191
19: 112,191
20: 56,191

# Short drive line: endpoint BEFORE the vertical edge at x≈180.
# Must project outward → t_end captures the crossing.
# Vertex 10 should land exactly on the vertical edge at x≈180.3.
drive-line
50,100
130,100
----
drive-line: 50,100 -> 130,100

graph precise
----
0: 0.0,0.0
1: 300.0,0.0
2: 300.0,200.0
3: 0.0,200.0
4: 279.1,0.0
5: 279.1,200.0
6: 223.3,0.0
7: 223.3,200.0
8: 167.5,0.0
9: 167.5,200.0
10: 111.6,0.0
11: 111.6,200.0
12: 55.8,-0.0
13: 55.8,200.0
14: 300.0,190.9
15: 0.0,190.9
16: 279.1,190.9
17: 223.3,190.9
18: 167.5,190.9
19: 111.6,190.9
20: 55.8,190.9
21: 0.0,100.0
22: 55.8,100.0
23: 111.6,100.0
24: 167.5,100.0

screenshot drive-line-intersection-short.png
----
